channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Anti-topological crystal and non-Abelian liquid in twisted semiconductor bilayers
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 25 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70916-z

The authors show that electron crystals compete closely with non-Abelian fractional Chern insulators in the half-full second moiré band of twisted bilayer MoTe2. In particular, they find an “antitopological” electron crystal with zero Chern number C arising because contributions to C from the full first band and half-full second band cancel.
item.dc:title: Anti-topological crystal and non-Abelian liquid in twisted semiconductor bilayers
item.dc:creator: Aidan P. Reddy
D. N. Sheng
Ahmed Abouelkomsan
Emil J. Bergholtz
Liang Fu

item.title: Beyond MPB engineering: enhanced piezoelectricity and thermal stability assisted by high-entropy design
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 25 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72335-6

The authors integrate morphotropic phase boundary engineering with high-entropy design in a quinary perovskite ceramic, which yields a piezoelectric coefficient of 580 pC/N and exceptional thermal stability, with performance retained up to 285 °C.
item.dc:title: Beyond MPB engineering: enhanced piezoelectricity and thermal stability assisted by high-entropy design
item.dc:creator: Al Momin Md Tanveer Karim
Ying Liu
John Daniels
Chunyang Gao
Yue Luo
Zhenzhu Cao
Xiaozhou Liao
Zhenxiang Cheng
Shujun Zhang
